code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test14-AUG-2015Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, low total CO₂, and significant proteinuria, suggesting possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals proteinuria, glucosuria, mild ketonuria, low specific gravity, and abnormal urine appearance (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal), but no evidence of infection (negative nitrites/leukocytes). Findings are consistent with renal dysfunction, possibly diabetic nephropathy or another glomerular pathology. Further nephrological evaluation is recommended.
